prompt: A HarborGate Visitor Access & Content Compliance system with main features including visitor entry authorization, on-premise attraction mapping, local notifications, and governance workflows for a venue or managed property. The application serves four roles: Regular User (visitor), Merchant/Operator (front-desk or tenant business), Reviewer (content/moderation staff), and Administrator (security and system owner). Users register and sign in locally, then create or view reservations that automatically generate time-bound entry permissions; each permission has a start/end window (default 15 minutes before to 30 minutes after the reservation), an entry policy (single-use consumes on first successful unlock; multi-use allows up to 5 entries), and a visible status badge in a Table view. Operators can trigger "Remote Unlock" from a Drawer panel for a selected door/device, with a confirmation Modal and a required reason note (min 10 characters) stored in the audit trail. A venue map screen manages attractions/POIs with manually entered coordinates (feet-based display), searchable by radius (e.g., within 1,500 ft), administrative zone (predefined locally), or polygon geofence drawn on a canvas overlay; route planning shows suggested entry points and estimated walk time using a configurable speed (default 3 mph) without relying on external map providers. A Notification Center inbox shows in-app reminders for approvals, overdue items, and missing materials; templates support variables (e.g., {reservationId}, {doorName}) and scheduled reminders (e.g., 24 hours and 1 hour before start time), with local retry rules (up to 3 attempts) and "delivered/failed" receipts within the UI. A CMS supports create-review-publish with optional multilingual variants and change diff/rollback, while the admin console handles reports with evidence chains, manual decisions, user bans, rate limits, and traceable change history. Built with native HTML/CSS/JavaScript as a single-page application using hash-based routing, the system runs fully in the browser with all state managed client-side. Persistence uses LocalStorage for small settings and sessions, and IndexedDB for primary records. Authentication is local-only with Web Crypto encryption at rest.
